details.text: Derivative: الفُرْقَانَ – The distinguisher and criterion which makes very clear the separation between good and evil and truth and falsehood.

- The ordering or sequence is atypical as the normal order would be: لِيَكُونَ نَذِيرًا لِلْعَالمِينَ. Advancing لِلْعَالمِينَ before نَذِيرًا is for the purpose of bringing attention to the fact that the Prophet ﷺ was sent to warn all nations, peoples and civilisations, whilst the prior Prophets had only been sent to their nations.

Derivative: قُصُور is the plural of قَصْر and means palaces or castles which is connected to the word قَيْصَر which means Caeser, referring to the ruler or emperor of Rome, and their castles were قُصُور. Here Allah is saying that He could have made the likes of huge Roman castles for you had He wished.
Derivative: تَغَيُّظًا refers to a furious raging roar
Derivative: زَفِير refers to a sound like exhaling or moaning that induces fear.

Derivative: ثُبور refers to a violent death: A wound to worsen until it is beyond recovery or to be destroyed in a way that he cannot recover from after that. Here, it means that the very moment they are cast into a part of it, they call or beg for a death and destruction that is final i.e., Please just kill me now so that I don’t have to suffer the punishment of hell.
Derivative: مَسْؤُولًا means to be asked for i.e., the believers asked and requested from Allah to grant them what He had promised in terms of reward and gardens of Paradise.
Derivative: بُورٌ means ruin and destruction and is specially related to the destruction or ruin of merchandise or land for farming i.e., that which wealth is acquired from.
Derivative: عَتَوْ from عَتأ – عُتُوٌ which means to behave in an insolent manner and transgress due to arrogance, pride and thinking too highly of oneself.
Derivative: حِجْرٌ is a barrier from something or something that is prohibited and is prevented from being acted upon or benefited from.

- There are different views concerning حِجْرًا مَّحْجُورًا. It is said that the disbelievers say this when they see the Angels to ward themselves from them and it was a saying of the Arabs that when they faced an impending adversity, they would say حِجْرًا مَّحْجُورًا to ward it off. Here it is to seek refuge from the Angels and impending punishment. Another view is that it is the Angels who say this to the disbelievers with the meaning that they will be prevented from entering Jannah and that it is Haram and Muharram (prohibited) for them.
Derivative: هَباءٌ means dust particles
Derivative: مَنْثُور means: Scattered or dispersed in every direction.
Derivative: مَقِيل comes from قَيْلُولَة which is a short afternoon rest. It means a place of rest and relaxation.
- The use of نُزِّلَ and تَنْزيل means that the مَلائكة (Angels) will descend in rows or groups one after another and not that they will descend in one go.
- To bite upon his hand is an expression used for someone experiencing great regret, anxiety and distress.
Derivative: خَليلٌ is a close friend.
Derivative: Someone who lets someone down and disassociates from him at the time of need. The one who shows his disloyalty when he is most needed.
Derivative: مَهْجُور is from هِجْرَة which means migration. مَهْجُورًا means to abandon or to leave in abandonment.

Derivative: فُؤَاد refers to the heart that is overwhelmed, for instance by fear, anger or excitement etc. Here, in the case of the Messenger ﷺ it can refer to his zeal and the increasing and strengthening of his tranquillity and perseverance.

- To make it recited in a slow, rhythmic and melodious manner. تَرْتِيل means to articulate every letter or word, to deliver it smoothly and to have measure or melody in the way speech is presented. Not only is the Qur’an not revealed all at once but even every small part of it should be taken time with. Another understanding of this statement is that it means that the Qur’an was revealed bit by bit where تَرْتِيل carries the meanings of not blending and passing into each other.
Derivative: يُحْشَرُونَ from حَشَرَ which means to gather, drive, to cram or press (together). Here it means to be driven upon their faces or dragged.
- أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا can mean the most astray in terms of following a path and also the most lost in terms of following a path.
Derivative: وَزِير is someone who helps someone in carrying his burden and assisting him.

- “And we destroyed them utterly or with a complete destruction”. The Mafhoom Al-Mutlaq (المَفْهُومُ المُطْلَق) here تَدْمِيرًا indicates to the complete and thorough destruction when the water came crushing down upon them.
Derivative: الرَّسّ is a name of a بِئْر (well).

Derivative: تَبَّرْنَا from تَبَّرَ – تَتْبِير means to destroy and annihilate. تَبَّرْنَا تَتْبِيرًا – We utterly destroyed them or completely annihilated them i.e., those nations that came before who disbelieved, denied the signs of Allah and ignored the warning.
Derivative: زُورًا a lie and falsehood that is claimed, similar to false propaganda.
Derivative: أَعَانَ means to provide a lot of help. عَوْنٌ means a great deal of help or assistance.
Derivative: إِلَٰهَ does only refer to a deity that is worshiped but also an entity that is obeyed and whose authority is accepted as absolute and submitted to.
Derivative: هَوَى – Empty desires or whims.
Derivative: سُبَات means rest, recovery and be at real peace.

Derivative: نُشُور – It can mean a means to be resurrected or to be revived after your rest and sleep. It can also mean to spread out i.e., after your rest you go out and disperse in the land to engage in your activities and earn your living.
Derivative: طَهُور does not just mean pure but rather purifying i.e., something that purifies.
- رَحْمَتِهِ “His mercy” here is used to refer to rain for which the winds and associated moving clouds are a glad tiding for its coming.

Derivative: صَرَّفَ can mean to make flow and here it can be talking about the rainwater. It can mean to alternate and diversify which could be related to the winds and the rain, just as it could be talking about the Qur’an and how the examples and signs are diversified.
Derivative: أَبَى to refuse adamantly. أَبَى followed by إِلَّا means to be bent and insistent upon something. It literally means “to refuse except to do or be something”.
Derivative: جِهَادًا from جَاهَدَ means to struggle against an opposing force with everything that you have.
Derivative: فُرَاتٌ – Very sweet and thirst quenching.
Derivative: بَرْزَخ is a barrier that can’t be crossed.
Derivative: عَذْبٌ is sweet water. It is the type of water that someone delights in drinking it.

the meaning of this verse “that there is a sweet water and a salty water that have an impenetrable barrier between them” can be applied to the reality of the quraish, following on from the struggle described in the previous verse, and the battle between the truth and falsehood, in the case where the barrier between the truth and falsehood must be maintained, so that falsehood does not enter.
- Impenetrable, completed barred and blocked off. Here each of the two waters “The sweet and the salty” have been prohibited from mixing with each other.

Derivative: ظَهِيرٌ can mean to have someone’s back or to turn your back on someone or something. ظَهِير عَلى means to turn one’s back and ظَهِيرٌ لِ means to have someone’s back. ظَهِيرٌ also is upon the فَعِيل form which is indicative of permanency. ظَهِير عَلى can also mean that he has the back of and supports someone against another. In this verse it would mean that the disbeliever has the back of Shaytan and assists him against His Rabb (Master).
Derivative: نُفُور means dislike and aversion. To increase in نُفُور means to make them flee and distance themselves further away from that which they have an aversion to.
Derivative: بُرُوجٌ literally means shiny towers. تَبَرَّجَ to display or show off beauty or charms. It is used to refer to brilliant or shiny stars which are seen in the sky.

Derivative: خِلْفَةً could be understood to mean that they are in conflict with one another in the case where they go back and forth against each other. It can also mean that they are successors to one another in the case where one follows the other.
Derivative: هَوْنًا means gently and tranquilly. It refers to a softness and humility in how the servants of Ar-Rahman walk upon the earth.
Derivative: خَاطَبَ - خِطاب means to address but in a public manner and out loud.

Derivative: غَرَامًا means something that sticks and is inseparable i.e., the punishment of hellfire is a natural attachment and consequence for the disbeliever and inescapable for him. غَرام can also refer to a loss or a penalty.
Derivative: ٱصْرِفْ – صَرَفَ عَن means to avert, divert away or to change the direction away.
Derivative: مُقَام is a permanent place of residence.
Derivative: مُسْتَقَرًّا is a place that is settled in and can be for a short or longer time.
Derivative: يَقْتُرُوا from قَتَرَ means to be over frugal and very unwilling to spend money or use resources.

Derivative: قَوَامًا literally means a straight and proper stature, state or stance and refers here to a middle way or measure and balanced way i.e., between قُتُور (tightness and over frugalness) and إٍسْراف (wasteful and over extravagant and in excess of limits).

Derivative: أَثَامًا – It can mean a great sin or error in the case here where he will come face to face with a great sin or error i.e., he would have perpetrated one. Or it can refer to the consequence and recompense for a إثْم (sin or misdeed) i.e., a punishment and penalty.
Derivative: مُهَانًا means to be in a state of humiliation.

Derivative: مَتَابًا is a مَصْدَر مِيمِي. It is a strong, true and accepted form of تَوْبَة (repentance). There are three words from تابَ (to repent) meaning repentance mentioned in the Qur’an: تَوْب, تَوْبَة and مَتَاب and مَتَاب is the strongest form of repentance of the three.
Derivative: يَشْهَدُونَ means to give testimony in the case where it means in this Verse: That they don’t give false testimony. Or it can also mean to witness or be in attendance to falsehood.

Derivative: يَخِرُّوا with عَلَى to fall over, or trip and stumble over. Here to fall over them in a state of deafness and blindness i.e., heedless and oblivious of the signs of Allah when they are reminded of them.
Derivative: إِمَامًا means a leader i.e., to the high level of being an example for the Muttaqun and a leader for them.

- قُرَّةَ أَعْيُن is an expression which means “the coolness of the eyes” and refers to peace of mind, joy and contentment – the delight of the eyes. It’s meaning here is to make our wives and offspring obedient to you and upon the straight path so that I can be at peace and content with them.
Derivative: غُرْفَة refers to every high, raised and lofty building and can refer here to an elevated level in Jannah.

Derivative: يَعْبَؤُا or يَعْبَأُ from عَبَاَ means to care or be concerned with or hold weight or value in something/someone. In origin it comes from عِبْءٌ which means something that has weight and is heavy or a burden (i.e., because of its weight – literal or metaphorical).

Derivative: لِزامًا means to become attached in an inevitable, binding and permanent manner i.e., Here, in this verse, it is referring to the punishment or your state of disbelief which will soon become stuck to you and become a permanent reality for you, from which there is no way back.
Derivative: مَسْحُور is literally someone who has been influenced by magic and was also used as an expression to refer to someone who had lost his mind.
